mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, causing investors to price in lower corporate earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0 percent over a rolling 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand across durable and non-durable sectors, which translates into higher corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, rapid expansion in industrial production reflects supply chain normalization and capacity utilization improvements, which drives short-term positive S&P 500 returns as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 outside recovery regimes or when global supply constraints persist for more than two quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding economic activity and higher aggregate consumer spending capacity, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth coincides with negative equity returns in greater than 60 percent of observations over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price indices exceed 240.0, it signals entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally with positive 60-day returns in greater than 70 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above 240.0.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising industrial production and falling unemployment creates a synchronized business cycle upswing, maximizing corporate profit margins and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns through earnings momentum.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when tested with a dummy variable for joint movement, or out-of-sample directional accuracy falls below 55 percent.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, elevated federal funds rates combined with slowing payroll growth signal policy-induced economic deceleration, triggering risk-off flows and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns when tested exclusively in late-cycle regimes, or the joint condition yields a positive Sharpe ratio for long equity positions.
